coredata.dc:title: Influence of ligand and impurities on optical properties of carbon dots: Narrow linewidth and controllable UV-Vis spectrum
coredata.dc:creator: Xu, Shuhong
Tan, Linfeng
Liu, Fan
Cui, Yiping
Shao, Haibao
Wang, Chunlei
Zhang, Rong
coredata.dc:description: Optical properties of carbon molecules have been investigated with DFT. First, the results show that the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum can be controlled by the number and the position of hydroxyl groups, while FWHM are only influenced by the position of hydroxyl groups. Second, study of molecules with different ligands (–Br, –OH, –COOH, –NH2) have confirmed that larger ligands would make the peaks wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum shift to red and make FWHM broaden. Finally, the doping of sulfur impurity would make the peaks wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum shift to longer wavelength than those doped with boron and nitrogen impurities.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Optical properties
FWHM
Ligand
Doping

Influence of number of hydroxyl groups on optical characters
Influence of ligand and impurity types on optical characters

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The configurations of pure carbon molecule and those with different position of hydroxyl group structures (three hydroxyl groups).
UV-Vis spectra for pure carbon molecule and those with different position of hydroxyl group molecules (three hydroxyl groups).
(a) The configurations of molecules with different position of hydroxyl groups (six hydroxyl groups); (b) and their UV-Vis spectrum.
Configures and UV-Vis spectra for molecules with –OH, –NH2, –COOH, and –Br as ligands.
The configurations of molecules with boron, nitrogen and sulfur impurities.
UV-Vis spectra for molecules with impurities boron, nitrogen and sulfur impurities.
Total energy (a.u.), FWHM (nm), Dipole moment (Debye) and symmetry of pure carbon molecule and those with different position of hydroxyl group molecules (three hydroxyl groups).

head.ce:title: Influence of ligand and impurities on optical properties of carbon dots: Narrow linewidth and controllable UV-Vis spectrum

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Influence of position and type of ligands on the optical characters of pure carbon molecule: UV-Vis spectrum and full width at half maxima.

Optical properties of carbon molecules have been investigated with DFT. First, the results show that the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum can be controlled by the number and the position of hydroxyl groups, while FWHM are only influenced by the position of hydroxyl groups. Second, study of molecules with different ligands (–Br, –OH, –COOH, –NH2) have confirmed that larger ligands would make the peaks wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum shift to red and make FWHM broaden. Finally, the doping of sulfur impurity would make the peaks wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum shift to longer wavelength than those doped with boron and nitrogen impurities.

ce:section.ce:para: Carbon dots (CDs) for good optical properties, small size, low cost and biocompatibility, have potential application in LED, catalysis and biomedicine etc. [1–15]. At present, the wavelength of Photoluminescence (PL) of CDs can be controlled in the whole visible range [16,17]. Moreover, some references have report the near infrared luminescence CDs [18,19]. Through study of optical properties of CDs, it is confirmed that there are two kinds of luminescent modes [20–26]. The first one is PL of CDs coming from defect or surface ligand. Full width at half maxima (FWHM) of spectra for this kind of luminescent mode is wider. Moreover, the wavelength of luminescent spectrum is changed according the excitation wavelength. Quantum size effect for this kind of CDs is not obviously. Namely, the wavelength of PL of this kind of CDs is not be regulated using size of CDs. The other luminescent mode is PL coming from core of CDs, which has clear quantum size effect and narrow FWHM. Besides, the wavelength of luminescent spectrum is not changed according the excitation wavelength. No matter which kind of luminescent mode, CDs has been used in many fields for its good optical properties. Besides, there are many reports to explore way to narrow FWHM of spectra. In 2018 [27], one kind of narrow FWHM emission CDs (FWHM 30 nm) was reported. In this paper, it is confirmed that the unique highly crystalline triangular structure functionalized with pure electron-donating hydroxyl groups at the edge sites showed highly delocalized charges, outstanding structural stability, and thus dramatically reduced electron-phonon coupling. Zhuang Y etc. reported an encapsulating-dissolving-recrystallization route for the synthesis of CD-based anti-counterfeiting inks with blue, green and red full-color afterglow emission in 2019 [28]. Recently, Yang B etc. developed a high-performance CDs with emission wavelength longer than 660 nm and with narrow FWHM, which has been used in vivo imaging performance in nude mice [15]. Sargent E etc. reported a kind of deep-blue light-emitting diodes (LEDs) based on narrow FWHM CDs which is obtained through amination of surface of CDs [29]. Thus, it can be seen that synthesizing of CDs with narrow FWHM is important for application of CDs in solid-state lighting. However, there is a few references to study the optical characters of CDs in theory. In 2014, Strauss et al. studied the optical absorption/emission properties of six possible structures with DFT [30]. Li et al. has explored the structure of CDs with oxygen and sulfur doping using DFT, but did not study the optical characters of these molecules [31]. Sheardy et al. using TDDFT investigated twelve different proposed structures to elucidate the nature of the absorptive properties. They focused on the internal functional groups [32]. Up to now, there is a few references to report the study of optical characters of carbon molecules with different ligand or impurities in theory. Moreover, the influence factors on FWHM have not been investigated or determined.

In this paper, the influence of configuration, ligand of surface and impurities on optical properties of CDs has been investigated in theory. The wavelength and FWHM of UV-Vis spectrum are related to the symmetry, the position of ligand, the number of ligand and the type of impurity. The theoretical data would be useful for experimental controlling the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum, and give guidance for the experiment obtaining CDs with narrower FWHM.

Gaussian 03 program was used to calculate the characters of carbon molecules [33]. Becke’s three-parameter hybrid exchange functional with the correlation functional by Lee et al. (B3LYP) 6-31G (d, p) basis set in DFT (density functional theory) calculation was chosen. Figures of geometric structures and MOs were generated using GaussView program [34]. UV-Vis spectra of these molecules were obtained with time-dependent DFT (TDDFT). It is common knowledge that UV-vis spectrum is a kind of absorption spectrum, refers to the spectrum produced by absorbing photons and transiting from low energy level to high energy level. Fluorescence emission or PL is excited when a substance absorbs light, which is photons transiting from high energy level to low energy level. Therefore, fluorescence emission is reverse process of absorption process. In experiment, the wavelength of fluorescence emission is shifting to red comparing with that of absorption spectrum for the influence of solvent and other factors, which is called Stockes shift. But in theory, the wavelength of fluorescence emission and absorption spectrum is the same.

From reference we have known that CDs with narrow FWHM emission can be prepared using phloroglucinol (PG) [27]. In experiment, three PG molecules formed three-fold symmetric PG triangulogen. Moreover, it has been confirmed that the molecular purity and crystalline perfection of the triangular CDs were the key to the high color-purity emission and narrow FWHM. Besides, reference has reported that ligand in surface of CDs would narrow FWHM of spectrum. For example, surface amination has been reported to be used to narrow FWHM [29]. Therefore, FWHM of spectrum would be influenced by the position and the type of surface ligand. We have chosen TDDFT to get UV-Vis spectra.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Influence of the position of ligands on optical characters
ce:section.ce:para: The structures composed of three PG molecules are designed, which are listing in Fig. 1 . All molecules have the same number of hydroxyl groups (three hydroxyl groups) except pure carbon molecule (namely structure 1-0), which have the same molecular formula, namely C18H9(OH)3. All carbon atoms in these structures are sp2 hybridized. Their difference is the position of three hydroxyl groups. The characters for these molecules are showed in Table 1 . Three hydroxyl groups in structure 1-1 are at the same position of benzene rings, which is C3h symmetry if all atoms at the same plane. Optimization results show that three benzene rings and hydroxyl groups are not at the same plane. The configuration of structure 1-2 has the same symmetry with structure 1-1. The moving of each hydroxyl group from structure 1-1 to structure 1-2 is identical. Structures 1-3, 1-4 and 1-5 are obtained from structure 1-1 through changing the position of one hydroxyl group. Obviously, structures 1-3, 1-4 and 1-5 have poor symmetries. The total energy shows that structure 1-2 has good stability for its lowest energy. Hydroxyl groups in structure 1-2 are far away compared with those in structure 1-1, which make structure 1-2 have small steric hindrance, resulting its good stability. Dipole moment of structure 1-2 is the lowest one among all these structures, except structure 1-0 without hydroxyl group. The structural stability of molecules relates to the position of hydroxyl groups.

Calculated UV-Vis spectra of six structures are showed in Fig. 2 . The absorption peaks of UV-Vis spectrum for them are 250 nm (1-0), 277 nm (1-1), 262 nm (1-2), 261 nm (1-3), 265 nm (1-4) and 279 nm (1-5). Judged by the absorption peaks, the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum of molecules with hydroxyl groups is shifting to longer wavelength, compared with that of without hydroxyl group. Therefore, hydroxyl group ligand in the surface of CDs would make UV-Vis spectra of CDs shift to longer wavelength. Molecules 1-1, 1-2, 1-3, 1-4 and 1-5 are isomers. The difference of them is the position of hydroxyl groups. Obviously, the wavelength of UV-Vis spectra for these isomers are different. Thus, for the same size CDs, the different position of ligands would produce different UV-Vis spectrum. Namely, the position of ligands would influence the wavelength of UV-Vis spectra of CDs. Moreover, molecules with larger steric hindrance would have longer peaks of wavelength. The results are agreed with experimental data. In experiment, using isomers (o-phenylenediamine (oPD), m-phenylenediamine (mPD), and p-phenylenedia-mine (pPD)) to synthesize CDs, the wavelength of UV-Vis spectra and PL are different, which may be caused by the different position of amino groups [35]. Besides, o, m, p-dihydroxybenzene molecules have been used to prepare CDs. The optical characters are different [36–38].

FWHM of pure carbon 1-0 is 38 nm, which is narrower than those of molecules with hydroxyl groups. Among these isomers, structure 1-2 has better symmetry and narrower FWHM (43 nm). The position of hydroxyl groups not only influences the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum, but also influences the width of FWHM. Moreover, structure 1-5 with larger steric hindrance have wider FWHM. Contrasting optical properties of pure carbon molecule and hydroxyl group doping molecules, it is confirmed that the adding of hydroxyl groups would make UV-Vis spectrum shift to longer wavelength and FWHM widen. For isomers with the same ligands, the position of hydroxyl groups would influence the wavelength and the width of FWHM of UV-Vis spectrum. Therefore, in experiment the best way to prepare CDs with short wavelength and narrow FWHM is using raw materials without ligand group. For CDs with ligands, we should reduce steric hindrance of ligand as little as possible to obtain CDs with the shorter wavelength emission and narrow FWHM.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Influence of number of hydroxyl groups on optical characters
ce:section.ce:para: Optical characterizations of structures with six hydroxyl groups are depicted in Fig. 3 . Fig. 3a is the configurations of two kinds of molecules. The difference between them is the position of one hydroxyl group (circling with a square). Clearly, structure 2-2 has larger steric hindrance than those of structure 2-1. FWHM of structure 2-1 is 50 nm, which is close to the data of those structures with three hydroxyl groups. Structure 2-2 with larger steric hindrance has wider FWHM (about 70 nm). From the above data, it is confirmed that the width of FWHM is influenced by the position of hydroxyl groups, which is identical with the above. The number of hydroxyl groups has little influence to FWHM. The wavelength of UV-Vis spectra for structures 2-1 and 2-2 is close. Hence, the influence of location of hydroxyl groups for the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum is not obvious for molecules with six hydroxyl groups. Besides, the peaks wavelength of UV-Vis spectra for structures 2-1 and 2-2 is 272 and 280 nm, which shift to long wavelength compared to pure carbon molecule 1-0. Moreover, the molecules with six hydroxyl groups have longer wavelength than those of molecules with three hydroxyl groups. In short, the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum of molecules is influenced by the position and the number of hydroxyl groups. The final wavelength is determined by both the position and the number of hydroxyl groups. FWHM in controlled by the position of hydroxyl groups, which has little relation with the number of hydroxyl group. Therefore, to get CDs with the shorter wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum, we should choose the structure with the smaller number of hydroxyl groups and lower steric hindrance. And CDs with narrow FWHM should be prepared using molecules with lower steric hindrance.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Influence of ligand and impurity types on optical characters
ce:section.ce:para: Except hydroxyl groups, we have investigated the influence of amino (–NH2), bromine (–Br) and carboxyl (–COOH) groups. Seen from Fig. 4 , the peaks of wavelength of UV-Vis spectra for structures with amino, bromine and carboxyl ligand groups are 285 nm, 269 nm and 268 nm, which is longer than that of pure carbon molecule (250 nm). The red shift of wavelength of molecules with amino and carboxyl groups is more than those with hydroxyl and bromine groups. Besides, FWHM for molecules with amino and carboxyl groups are 50 nm. And those for molecules with hydroxyl and bromine groups are about 43 nm. Obviously, the types of ligand would influence both the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum and the width of FWHM. In general, the larger ligand would result in bigger red shift and wider FWHM. Therefore, using molecules with smaller ligand would be better to obtain CDs with shorter emission and narrow FWHM. Electronic structure of molecules with hydroxyl, amino, bromine and carboxyl groups have been studied. Molecular orbital of molecules with different ligand groups are listing as Supporting information S1. Highest occupied molecular orbital (HOMO) and lowest unoccupied molecular orbital (LUMO) was calculated using HF/6-31G (d, p), showed with GaussView. Seen from Fig. S1, it is confirmed that small ligands such as –Br and –OH have little influence to the delocalization of benzene ring. Thus, the influence of small ligands (–Br and –OH) on UV-vis spectrum is less than those of large ligands (–NH2 and –COOH). Besides, optical characters of molecules with different location Br atoms have been investigated. UV-vis spectra of structures with Br are depicted in Fig. S2. Obviously, influence of Br on the structures has much different with that with hydroxyl groups, which is maybe caused by the large size of Br atoms. However, for molecules with Br atom, the rule of UV-vis spectrum and FWHM is similar to that of molecules with hydroxyl groups.

For CDs, their optical characters are related to impurities of boron, nitrogen and sulfur. Fig. 5 shows structures with boron, nitrogen and sulfur impurities. For boron and nitrogen doping, there is three model with different positions of boron and nitrogen atoms. And there are two models for sulfur doping. UV-Vis spectrum of B1 structure have two peaks, which is different from N1 structure. B2, B3 and N2, N3 have similar optical characters. The wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum of B doping molecules shift to red, compared to those of N doping molecules. Sulfur doping molecule S2 has longer wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum. From the above data, it is confirmed that B doping structure as B1 models would obtain longer wavelength for CDs. Spectra of N doping CDs would have the same rule with B doping one. As for sulfur doping, doping as model S2 would obtain longer wavelength, which is agreed with experimental results [39]. Molecular orbital of molecules with boron and nitrogen impurities are showed in Fig. S3. From HOMO and LUMO, we have found structures with impurities B and N have different bonding properties. Nitrogen atom has redundant pairs of electrons to form bonding. B3 and N3 have better delocalization than those of other structures Fig. 6 .

In this paper, we have studied optical characters of molecules with different ligand position, number and type. First, the results show that the wavelength of UV-Vis spectra of CDs is influenced by the position and the number of hydroxyl groups. The final wavelength is determined by both the position and the number of hydroxyl groups. FWHM in controlled by the position of hydroxyl groups. Second, molecules with larger ligand for amino and carboxyl groups have longer wavelength and wide FWHM than those of molecules with smaller ligand bromine and hydroxyl groups. Finally, optical characters of pure carbon with impurities of boron, nitrogen and sulfur study confirm that sulfur impurity, doping as model S2 would obtain longer wavelength. Therefore, to obtain CDs with narrow FWHM, the best way is preparing CDs with pure carbon molecule. Small ligand and the position of ligand with good symmetry would be benefit for preparing CDs with narrow FWHM. Doping sulfur into CDs would be advantageous to prepare CDs with longer wavelength. Controlling the position and type of ligand of CDs would adjust the wavelength of UV-Vis spectrum. The data obtained in theory would be useful for experimental investigation.

Shuhong Xu: Software, Conceptualization, Writing - original draft, Supervision, Writing - review & editing. Linfeng Tan: Supervision, Writing - review & editing. Fan Liu: Supervision, Writing - review & editing. Yiping Cui: Software, Conceptualization, Writing - original draft, Supervision, Writing - review & editing. Haibao Shao: Writing - original draft, Supervision, Writing - review & editing. Chunlei Wang: Writing - original draft, Supervision, Writing - review & editing. Rong Zhang: Writing - original draft, Supervision, Writing - review & editing.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This work is supported by the Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 61535003), the Natural Science Foundation of China (Grant Nos. 22075043, 21875034, 61704093), the Foundation of Jiangsu Province for Outstanding Young Teachers in University (Grant No. BK20180064).

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para: Supplementary data to this article can be found online at https://doi.org/10.1016/j.cplett.2021.138520.

ce:section.ce:label: Appendix A
ce:section.ce:para:
The following are the Supplementary data to this article:
Supplementary data 1
